description:
You are an environmental restoration planner assessing erosion potential along the White River in Muncie, Indiana. Using the Muncie HEC-RAS unsteady simulation output (Muncie.p04.hdf) located in ~/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie, write and execute a Python script named compute_stream_power.py in ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/ to compute the stream power profile at the time of peak discharge. 

The analysis must identify the time step where peak flow occurs at the most downstream cross-section (the smallest river station value). At this peak time, calculate the Water Surface Slope (S) between consecutive cross-sections ($S_i = |WSE_{i+1} - WSE_i| / |Station_{i+1} - Station_i|$) and the Stream Power per unit length ($\Omega = \gamma \cdot Q \cdot S$, where $\gamma = 62.4$ lb/ft³). Export the profile data to ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/stream_power_profile.csv with columns: Station, Flow_cfs, WSE_ft, WS_Slope, and StreamPower. Additionally, generate a summary file at ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/stream_power_summary.txt containing the Max_StreamPower_Station, Max_StreamPower_Value, and Peak_Flow_cfs.
